channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Grants Docking Permission to Iranian Ships, IRIS Lavan Arrives in Kochi
item.description: India has officially approved Iran’s request for docking three of its ships, with IRIS Lavan being the first to dock at Kochi port, as confirmed by a spokesperson from the Ministry of External Affairs...

item.title: Sunrisers Leeds acquire Abrar Ahmed despite Pakistan-India cricket tensions
item.description: Pakistani leg-spinner Abrar Ahmed has been signed by Sunrisers Leeds for the 2026 edition of The Hundred, a 100-ball cricket competition in England and Wales, despite concerns about political tensions...

item.title: Understanding Skin Mites: Natural Residents of Healthy Skin
item.description: Almost all humans carry microscopic mites on their skin, which live within hair follicles and sebaceous glands, feeding on skin oils and dead cells. These mites, primarily Demodex folliculorum and Dem...

item.title: 16-Year-Old Liana France Becomes Australia's Youngest Female Winter Paralympian
item.description: Sixteen-year-old Liana France has made history as Australia’s youngest female Winter Paralympian during the Milano Cortina 2026 Games. Making her debut in the women’s giant slalom standing event, Fran...

item.title: High Court Releases Man Detained for Allegedly Damaging Currency Notes
item.description: The High Court in Kisumu has directed the immediate release of Maximillan Motai Nyagwaya, who had been held for allegedly mutilating Kenyan currency notes. Justice Joe Omido ruled that Nyagwaya's cont...

item.title: Defensor and 16 Former Marines Appear Before NBI Over Bribery Allegations
item.description: On March 12, 2026, former military personnel, including a man identified as Defensor, responded to a subpoena issued by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) regarding allegations of bribery. A t...

item.title: Global Oil Supplies Disrupted as Iran Conflict Escalates
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran has triggered severe disruptions in global oil supplies, prompting emergency measures from nations worldwide. According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), th...

item.title: Investigation finds Canadian horses exported to Japan for slaughter face illness, injury, and death
item.description: A recent investigation by Animal Justice, in collaboration with Japan-based Life Investigation Agency (LIA), has revealed troubling conditions for Canadian horses exported to Japan for slaughter. Usin...

item.title: Ghana plans UN resolution to seek reparations for transatlantic slavery
item.description: Ghana is set to propose a resolution at the United Nations recognising the transatlantic slave trade as the 'gravest crime in the history of humankind' and calling for reparations. The West African na...

item.title: Temporary Ceasefire at Torkham Allows Attempt to Retrieve Afghan National's Body Amid Ongoing Border Clashes
item.description: On Thursday, border forces from Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a short pause in their ongoing clashes at the Torkham crossing in Khyber to identify and retrieve a dead body lying at Zero Point for...

item.title: Republican allies push Trump to maintain aggressive migrant deportation policies despite internal concerns
item.description: The article describes growing tensions inside President Donald Trump’s political and governing coalition over how aggressively his administration should pursue migrant deportations. A group of hardlin...

item.title: DRC confirms maize market for Tanzanian farmers following quality inspection
item.description: Tanzanian farmers are now assured of a steady market for their maize after a delegation from the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) visited the country to check on maize stocks and their quality. The ...

item.title: UEFA Investigates Chelsea’s Pedro Neto Over Incident With Ball Boy
item.description: UEFA don begin disciplinary process against Chelsea winger, Pedro Neto, after some wahala wey happen with one ball boy during Chelsea’s heavy 5-2 loss to Paris Saint-Germain for Champions League last-...

item.title: Delta State, NDDC and Chevron to Collaborate on Completion of Omadino–Okerenkoko–Escravos Road and Bridges Project
item.description: The Delta State Government, the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC), and Chevron Nigeria Limited are joining forces to push forward the long-stalled Omadino–Okerenkoko–Escravos road and bridges ...

item.title: England records highest proportion of delayed cancer diagnoses in two years
item.description: In January 2026, NHS England experienced its worst performance in cancer diagnosis over the past two years. New data indicates that 27.2% of suspected cancer patients waited longer than four weeks to ...

item.title: ISRO Successfully Conducts Sea-Level Test of CE20 Engine at 22-Tonne Thrust
item.description: BENGALURU: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) on Thursday announced the successful sea-level hot test of its CE20 cryogenic engine, which generated a thrust of 22 tonnes, at the ISRO Propul...

item.title: Liam Delivers Heartfelt Apology to Victims of La Playa Roja Siege in Roja Episode 79
item.description: In the March 12, 2026 episode of the ABS-CBN action-drama series 'Roja' (Episode 79), the character Liam, portrayed by Donny Pangilinan, takes a significant emotional step by publicly expressing his d...

item.title: High Court Petition Challenges Legality of Francis Meja’s PSC Appointment
item.description: Nairobi — The appointment of Francis Meja as the chairperson of Kenya's Public Service Commission (PSC) is now under legal scrutiny. A petition filed at the High Court by Magare Gikenyi and Eliud Kara...

item.title: Ambassador Mighty Ogunleye Honoured for Leadership and Youth Empowerment
item.description: On Thursday, March 12, 2026, Amb. Mighty Ogunleye, Chairman of Mighty Group, was recognised by the West Africa Youth Council (WAYC) with the Nelson Mandela Leadership Award for his exceptional leaders...

item.title: Lobbyists Push to Shield Oil Companies from Climate Change Lawsuits
item.description: The article highlights ongoing efforts by the fossil fuel industry and its political allies to prevent legal accountability for climate-related damages. Across the U.S., including California, state an...
